stub: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background.
constituency: She is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: She rarely speaks out and votes against her party leadership.
substantive: She supports the construction of thousands of new onshore wind turbines.
justification: In political debates, she regularly explains how her policies matter to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background).
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I went into politics to implement good ideas, not popular ones that win elections.”
text: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background. She is from a constituency that is not your constituency. She rarely speaks out and votes against her party leadership. She supports the construction of thousands of new onshore wind turbines. In political debates, she regularly explains how her policies matter to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background). Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I went into politics to implement good ideas, not popular ones that win elections.”
